mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization improvements and supply-side stabilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds are followed by negative 5-day returns in greater than 60% of observed instances, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent while unemployment declines, accommodative policy successfully stimulates economic activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contractions exceeding 1.0 percent indicate severe aggregate demand destruction, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of periods following GDP contractions below -1.0%, or mean return difference is not significant at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms income-driven consumption expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 20-day horizons, or predictive R-squared remains below 0.01 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real corporate margins and raises expectations for monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 58% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample directional accuracy tests.
